title: Xiphotheca axillaris
intro: Xiphotheca axillaris, also known as the "River Bushwillow", is a small, deciduous tree native to the eastern and southern parts of Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Xiphotheca axillaris include flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Xiphotheca axillaris is often used in traditional medicine to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly administered orally to treat a range of health problems.
